title: Avery Dennison (AVY) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Avery Dennison (AVY) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 67/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 5.0% FCF growth rate is projected. This aligns with historical performance and accounts for steady materials growth coupled with the higher-margin, faster-growing intelligent labels (RFID) segment offsetting cyclical apparel weakness.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.0% discount rate is utilized, balancing the company's relatively stable cash flows and manageable debt load with its exposure to broader macroeconomic and retail cycles.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate reflects long-term, mature industrial growth slightly trailing global GDP, acknowledging the physical limitations of scaling materials production indefinitely.

thesisParagraphs: Avery Dennison is widely recognized as a stalwart in the traditional pressure-sensitive materials space, often perceived as a slow-growing legacy industrial business. However, this perception overlooks its structural shift toward high-tech, intelligent labeling solutions.

Through its expanding RFID technology portfolio, AVY is embedding itself deeper into global supply chains. As retailers and logistics providers demand higher visibility, item-level tracking, and automated inventory management, AVY's smart labels provide critical infrastructure. This segment offers faster growth and higher margins than its traditional adhesives. While cyclicality in the apparel sector (a key end-market) remains a short-term headwind, the long-term secular trend toward connected packaging and digital IDs underpins a reliable expansion of free cash flow, justifying a measured premium to its historical valuation.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini assign a 5.0% growth rate for Avery Dennison?
faq.answer: While the core materials segment grows at a slower, GDP-like pace, the rapid expansion of its high-margin Intelligent Labels (RFID) business elevates the overall blended growth rate, justifying a 5.0% projection.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for AVY's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.0% discount rate was applied. It represents Avery Dennison's stable cash generation as an established industrial leader, offset by inherent cyclical risks tied to the consumer retail sector.

faq.question: What is the primary catalyst for Avery Dennison?
faq.answer: The primary growth driver is the broader adoption of RFID and smart label technologies beyond retail apparel, expanding into massive new markets like logistics, food safety, and healthcare supply chains.

prospectVerdictDetail: Avery Dennison continues to hold a strong market position as a leading global manufacturer of pressure-sensitive adhesive materials and apparel branding labels. The company's expansion into RFID and smart labels provides a robust growth engine that modernizes its traditional labeling business. Steady free cash flow and a healthy balance sheet provide flexibility for shareholder returns and strategic acquisitions. While cyclical pressures in the apparel and retail sectors present near-term headwinds, AVY's durable competitive advantages make it a solid long-term prospect.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: AVY displays steady competitive momentum, largely supported by its market-leading position in materials science and its growing intelligent labels segment.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Growth is steady rather than spectacular, characteristic of the industrial materials sector. However, its Intelligent Labels business (RFID) consistently outpaces core materials growth, driving a better top-line mix.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company maintains dominant market share in pressure-sensitive materials and apparel tags. It continues to capture additional share in the rapidly expanding retail RFID space.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Avery Dennison exhibits solid pricing power due to the critical, yet low-cost nature of its products relative to the final consumer good. This enables the company to effectively pass on raw material inflation to its customers.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Innovation is highly focused on sustainability and smart technology. The continuous rollout of new RFID inlays and eco-friendly adhesive solutions demonstrates a steady, though incremental, product velocity.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The economic moat is founded on scale, technological expertise in materials science, and deeply embedded customer relationships across global supply chains.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: For massive retail and logistics clients, switching label and RFID providers carries significant operational friction and risk of supply chain disruption, creating sticky customer relationships.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: While not a traditional platform, there is a mild network effect in the RFID ecosystem: as more retailers adopt AVY's standards, suppliers are highly incentivized to use the same technology.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The company holds a vast portfolio of patents in materials science, adhesives, and RFID design. This IP forms a massive barrier to entry for smaller competitors trying to match its product quality.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Manufacturing at global scale requires capital, but AVY has optimized its footprint to be highly efficient. It reliably generates strong cash flow relative to its capital expenditures.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment is generally stable, reflecting the company's reliability and consistent dividend growth, though cyclical retail concerns occasionally weigh on the stock.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates remain relatively stable, supported by steady demand in logistics and a gradual recovery in apparel destocking. Margin expansion initiatives are a key focus for analysts.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative centers around the transition from a traditional materials company to an 'intelligent labels' tech player. The news is broadly positive regarding their sustainability goals and technological adoption.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has a long track record of disciplined capital allocation, characterized by reliable dividend increases, strategic tuck-in acquisitions, and consistent share repurchases.

prospectKeyRisks: High exposure to consumer discretionary spending; downturns in retail and apparel heavily impact the branding and information solutions segment.
Volatility in raw material costs, particularly petrochemicals, can squeeze gross margins if price increases lag behind cost inflation.
Intense competition in the highly fragmented global materials and packaging market could pressure long-term growth and pricing power.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Food RFID adoption reaching inflection point could double AVY's RFID addressable market from $10B to $20B+, driven by waste reduction mandates and inventory accuracy requirements
Walmart's RFID mandate expansion beyond apparel into additional categories creates a ripple effect across the entire retail supply chain, accelerating adoption across AVY's customer base
Sustainability-driven demand for recyclable label materials and digital product passports (EU requirements) creates new growth vectors for AVY's materials science and digital ID capabilities
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored AVY at 71/100 and Opus at 67/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
